lines.english: Lesson 11
lines.chinese: 第11课
lines.raw: Lesson 11 | 第11课

lines.english: How to grow old
lines.chinese: 如何安度晚年
lines.raw: How to grow old | 如何安度晚年

lines.english: What, according to the author, is the best way to overcome the fear of death as you get older?
lines.chinese: 照作者看来，随着年事增高，克服对死亡的恐惧的最佳方法是什么？
lines.raw: What, according to the author, is the best way to overcome the fear of death as you get older? | 照作者看来，随着年事增高，克服对死亡的恐惧的最佳方法是什么？

lines.english: Some old people are oppressed by the fear of death.
lines.chinese: 有些老年人害怕死亡。
lines.raw: Some old people are oppressed by the fear of death. | 有些老年人害怕死亡。

lines.english: In the young there is a justification for this feeling.
lines.chinese: 这种情绪如果发生在年轻人身上，是情有可原的。
lines.raw: In the young there is a justification for this feeling. | 这种情绪如果发生在年轻人身上，是情有可原的。

lines.english: Young men who have reason to fear that they will be killed in battle
lines.chinese: 青年人有理由担心他们会战死沙场，
lines.raw: Young men who have reason to fear that they will be killed in battle | 青年人有理由担心他们会战死沙场，

lines.english: may justifiably feel bitter in the thought
lines.chinese: 想到他们没有享受到人生最美好的东西，
lines.raw: may justifiably feel bitter in the thought | 想到他们没有享受到人生最美好的东西，

lines.english: that they have been cheated of the best things that life has to offer.
lines.chinese: 因而感到痛苦，这也是情有可原的。
lines.raw: that they have been cheated of the best things that life has to offer. | 因而感到痛苦，这也是情有可原的。

lines.english: But in an old man who has known human joys and sorrows,
lines.chinese: 但是，一个已经尝过人间甘苦的老人，
lines.raw: But in an old man who has known human joys and sorrows, | 但是，一个已经尝过人间甘苦的老人，

lines.english: and has achieved whatever work it was in him to do,
lines.chinese: 并且已经做完了他力所能及的一切工作，
lines.raw: and has achieved whatever work it was in him to do, | 并且已经做完了他力所能及的一切工作，

lines.english: the fear of death is somewhat abject and ignoble.
lines.chinese: 如果他仍然害怕死亡，那就有几分可怜又可鄙了。
lines.raw: the fear of death is somewhat abject and ignoble. | 如果他仍然害怕死亡，那就有几分可怜又可鄙了。

lines.english: The best way to overcome it--so at least it seems to me--
lines.chinese: 克服这种恐惧的最好办法——至少在我看来是如此——
lines.raw: The best way to overcome it--so at least it seems to me-- | 克服这种恐惧的最好办法——至少在我看来是如此——

lines.english: is to make your interests gradually wider and more impersonal,
lines.chinese: 是使你的兴趣逐渐扩大，变得更加超然物外，
lines.raw: is to make your interests gradually wider and more impersonal, | 是使你的兴趣逐渐扩大，变得更加超然物外，

lines.english: until bit by bit the walls of the ego recede,
lines.chinese: 直到自我的界限一点点地消退，
lines.raw: until bit by bit the walls of the ego recede, | 直到自我的界限一点点地消退，

lines.english: and your life becomes increasingly merged in the universal life.
lines.chinese: 你的生命越来越融入到普遍的生命之中。
lines.raw: and your life becomes increasingly merged in the universal life. | 你的生命越来越融入到普遍的生命之中。

lines.english: An individual human existence should be like a river--
lines.chinese: 人的个体生命应该像一条河流——
lines.raw: An individual human existence should be like a river-- | 人的个体生命应该像一条河流——

lines.english: small at first, narrowly contained within its banks,
lines.chinese: 最初很小，被狭窄的河岸限制着，
lines.raw: small at first, narrowly contained within its banks, | 最初很小，被狭窄的河岸限制着，

lines.english: and rushing passionately past boulders and over waterfalls.
lines.chinese: 汹涌地冲过巨石，飞跃瀑布。
lines.raw: and rushing passionately past boulders and over waterfalls. | 汹涌地冲过巨石，飞跃瀑布。

lines.english: Gradually the river grows wider, the banks recede, the waters flow more quietly,
lines.chinese: 渐渐地，河流变得宽阔，河岸后退，水流也变得更加平静，
lines.raw: Gradually the river grows wider, the banks recede, the waters flow more quietly, | 渐渐地，河流变得宽阔，河岸后退，水流也变得更加平静，

lines.english: and in the end, without any visiblebreak, they become merged in the sea,
lines.chinese: 最终，在没有任何明显的断裂的情况下，它们汇入了大海，
lines.raw: and in the end, without any visiblebreak, they become merged in the sea, | 最终，在没有任何明显的断裂的情况下，它们汇入了大海，

lines.english: and painlessly lose their individual being.
lines.chinese: 并且毫无痛苦地失去了它们的个体存在。
lines.raw: and painlessly lose their individual being. | 并且毫无痛苦地失去了它们的个体存在。

lines.english: The man who, in old age can see his life in this way,
lines.chinese: 那些年老时能以这种方式看待自己生命的人，
lines.raw: The man who, in old age can see his life in this way, | 那些年老时能以这种方式看待自己生命的人，

lines.english: will not suffer from the fear of death,
lines.chinese: 将不会受到对死亡的恐惧的折磨，
lines.raw: will not suffer from the fear of death, | 将不会受到对死亡的恐惧的折磨，

lines.english: since the things he cares for will continue.
lines.chinese: 因为他所珍视的一切将会继续存在。
lines.raw: since the things he cares for will continue. | 因为他所珍视的一切将会继续存在。

lines.english: And if, with the decay of vitality, weariness increases,
lines.chinese: 而且，如果随着活力的衰退，疲劳感增加，
lines.raw: And if, with the decay of vitality, weariness increases, | 而且，如果随着活力的衰退，疲劳感增加，

lines.english: the thought of rest will be not unwelcome.
lines.chinese: 休息的念头将不会令人不快。
lines.raw: the thought of rest will be not unwelcome. | 休息的念头将不会令人不快。

lines.english: I should wish to die while still at work,
lines.chinese: 我希望在工作时死去，
lines.raw: I should wish to die while still at work, | 我希望在工作时死去，

lines.english: knowing that others will carry on what I can no longer do,
lines.chinese: 知道别人会继续我无法再做的事情，
lines.raw: knowing that others will carry on what I can no longer do, | 知道别人会继续我无法再做的事情，

lines.english: and content in the thought that what was possible has been done.
lines.chinese: 并满意地想到，所有可能做的事情都已经完成了。
lines.raw: and content in the thought that what was possible has been done. | 并满意地想到，所有可能做的事情都已经完成了。
